title: iris spuria subsp. musulmanica
intro: Iris spuria subsp. musulmanica, also known as the Eastern flax-leaved iris or Persian iris, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the iris family (Iridaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Iris spuria subsp. musulmanica are attributed to its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Iris spuria subsp. musulmanica can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being.
